---
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: false

execute:
  cache: false
  echo: false
  warnings: false
  error: false
---

```{r}
#| results: "asis"
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
source("../../_common.R")
status(type = "drafting")
```


# Overview {.unnumbered}

## Introduction {.unnumbered}

This chapter presents data on antibiotic sales for humans in Sweden, describing total sales, sales by healthcare sector and trends over time. Together with data on resistance patterns, these findings support exploring the relationship between antibiotic use and antimicrobial resistance in humans.

---

## Key Findings {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip}
- In 2025, total sales of antibiotics for humans decreased by 2.8% compared with 2024.
- Antibiotic sales decreased in outpatient care, including dentistry. However, sales of antibiotics in inpatient care increased.
- Access group antibiotics made up 77% of all antibiotic sales (J01 excluding methenamine J01XX05).   
:::

---
